question: Looking at the US unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The highlighted states show a wide range of unemployment rate levels.
options.B: Idaho and Virginia have similar unemployment rates.
options.C: The unemployment rate gap between Idaho and Virginia might exceed that between New Jersey and Mississippi.
options.D: The unemployment rate differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: Idaho (11.7%) and Virginia (17.6%) have a 5.9% unemployment rate difference, while New Jersey (7.6%) and Mississippi (8.4%) have only a 0.8% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Idaho and Virginia into the same color bin (10%+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while New Jersey and Mississippi appear more different due to different color bins.
